title: eremophila forrestii subsp. capensis
intro: Eremophila forrestii subsp. capensis, also known as Forrest's emu bush, is a shrub native to Western Australia. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties due to the presence of iridoids, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The iridoids in this plant have been associated with potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce the risk of infections. Eremophila forrestii subsp. capensis is typically prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
